refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to operators and system managers.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate real-time packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
Dependency on real-time monitoring data for adaptation.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically encapsulates the solution to maintain packet loss within limits with minimal coupling to other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating latency monitoring as part of the adaptive protocol to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware components responsible for teleoperation communication and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics to other system modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands and tasks.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope, but less focused containment of teleoperation communication logic.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical actuation, less suitable for communication protocol logic.
Highest containment score; module is dedicated to teleoperation communication and control.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety policies, not communication protocol.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication protocol logic, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Network Link interface responsibilities managed elsewhere.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for safety-critical communication alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Coordinate interface definitions with Network Link and Safety Services to ensure clear boundaries and responsibilities.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear performance criteria and measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if adaptive protocol logic is partially implemented outside Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for the DP, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance coordination with Safety Services for safety-critical communication alerts.
Review latency impact of retransmission strategies within Teleoperation module.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting containment and coupling criteria. Verification and ownership are well defined.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to operators and system managers.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; next step is to decompose sub-FRs for detailed design.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Latency impact of retransmission is acceptable or manageable.
open_risks: Integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
Potential latency increase affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
evidence_needed: Packet loss measurements under representative network conditions.
Latency impact analysis of adaptive communication protocol.
